prompt:
Ticket from the edge-runtime team. Our agent needs a tiny embedded KV store for its config cache — bolt/badger are overkill and the edge builds are stdlib-only. Module go-kvwal, package kvwal, this repo.

Storage model: exactly one append-only write-ahead log at <dir>/wal.log. Every mutation appends a record; the in-memory map is rebuilt by replaying the log on Open. The on-disk framing is fixed because the recovery tests poke at the file directly: each record is a 4-byte big-endian payload length, then a 4-byte big-endian IEEE CRC32 of the payload, then the payload bytes. What goes inside the payload (op tag, key/value encoding) is your design.

API:
- Open(dir string) (*Store, error) — creates wal.log if missing, replays it otherwise. If the tail is torn or garbage (declared length runs past EOF, CRC mismatch, or the payload won't decode), keep every record before the bad one, truncate the file to that valid prefix right there in Open, and carry on. A writer that died mid-append must never brick the store.
- Put(key string, value []byte) error — empty key is an error and stores nothing. Overwrite = append a new record; last record for a key wins on replay. Defensive copies both ways: a caller reusing its value buffer, or scribbling on a slice Get returned, must not corrupt the store.
- Get(key string) ([]byte, bool). Zero-length values are legal and distinct from missing.
- Delete(key string) error — appends a tombstone; the key stays dead across reopens. Deleting a missing key is a no-op, not an error. A Put after a tombstone resurrects the key.
- Keys() []string (live keys, sorted ascending) and Len() int (live key count).
- Compact() error — rewrite the log to just the live state (no tombstones, no superseded versions) and swap it in atomically: build a temp file, rename it over wal.log, never leave a half-written log as the real one. Appends must keep working after a compact.
- Close() error — Put/Delete/Compact after Close return errors.

The store is hit from multiple goroutines and CI runs -race, so make it safe. kvwal_test.go and recovery_test.go are the acceptance contract — do not touch them; go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass. Structure it like a real package: store logic, record encode/replay, and compaction in separate files, not one giant blob.
